channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India's Tata, JSW Invest $1 Billion to Build EV Battery Power, Challenge China
item.description: Indian conglomerates Tata Group and JSW Group are investing nearly $1 billion to boost domestic electric vehicle (EV) and battery technology. The move aims to reduce dependence on Chinese suppliers by developing its own expertise in key battery types like Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) and Lithium Manganese Iron Phosphate (LiMFP). This investment highlights a major industry trend toward local innovation and stronger supply chains in India's fast-growing EV market, which saw sales jump nearly 70% in early 2026.

item.title: Bengal Govt Plans Capex Rise Amid Spending Pressures
item.description: West Bengal's new government aims to boost capital spending by about three percentage points, following a trend in states with new leadership. But this planned increase faces major challenges. The state's current capital expenditure, 13% of its total budget, trails the national average (16.2%) and leaders like Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh (nearly 28%). Recent budgets show revenue spending growing faster than investment, with capital spending actually falling. This raises doubts about the state's ability to fix its infrastructure issues despite election promises.

item.title: Core Scientific Suffers $347M Loss as AI Pivot Relies on One Client
item.description: Core Scientific posted a $347.2 million loss in Q1 on $115.2 million revenue. This stemmed from a $266.5 million asset write-down and bitcoin sales. The company is rapidly shifting to AI colocation, now its main business, earning $77.5 million. This pivot is backed by $3.3 billion in new debt but heavily depends on one client for 67% of its revenue, creating significant financial risk.

item.title: CG Power Target Hiked on Expansion, But High Valuation Risks Persist
item.description: Motilal Oswal Financial Services has reiterated a 'Buy' rating and boosted its price target for CG Power and Industrial Solutions Ltd. to ₹940, signaling a potential 13% upside. The bullish stance is anchored by exceptional performance in the power systems division, aggressive capacity expansion, and a robust order book. However, the company's semiconductor (OSAT) business continues to weigh on consolidated margins, and its elevated valuation multiples are drawing increased scrutiny from market analysts.

item.title: India Equity Funds Deliver Big Gains Amid Concentrated Risks
item.description: Indian equity funds focused on infrastructure, mid, and small caps have delivered strong growth over five years, often beating other investments. Their concentrated approach, however, means higher volatility, making them best for long-term investors who can handle risk. Demand for these themed funds remains high, backed by steady domestic investments, but investors must weigh the significant concentration risks.

item.title: Tata Sons IPO Forced by RBI Rules; Trust Dispute Flares, SP Group Sees Opportunity
item.description: Tata Sons must go public by July 1, 2026, due to new RBI rules for large non-banking finance firms. This regulatory push has revealed a rift within the Tata Trusts board, with trustees Venu Srinivasan and Vijay Singh favoring the IPO, clashing with Noel Tata who prefers keeping the company private. The IPO could unlock substantial value for minority holders like the Shapoorji Pallonji Group and challenge Noel Tata's leadership plans.

item.title: Awfis Adds 1.14 Lakh Sq Ft in Chennai for Large Businesses
item.description: Flexible workspace provider Awfis has launched two new premium centers in Chennai, adding 1.14 lakh sq. ft. to its 28-center network. This expansion targets the surging demand from large enterprises, which now dominate Chennai's ~8.5 million sq. ft. flex market, seeking agility and cost efficiency. Awfis's 'Gold' centers and efficient operational model position it to capture this high-value segment amidst market consolidation and evolving workplace strategies.

item.title: CarTrade Tech Hits Record FY26 Profit, But Valuation Worries Linger
item.description: CarTrade Tech posted its best-ever fiscal year (FY26) with revenue up 22% to Rs 870 crore and net profit soaring 68% to Rs 244 crore. Strong results came from its consumer, remarketing, and classifieds businesses. However, investors are watching the company's high valuation and growing competition in the digital auto market closely.

item.title: Cars24 Hits First Profit Milestone Driven by AI Ecosystem
item.description: Auto-tech firm Cars24 achieved its first-ever quarterly profit of Rs 20 crore in Q4 FY26, a significant milestone driven by AI-powered ecosystem growth and expansion across lending, retail sales, and ownership services. The company's strategic shift towards a comprehensive car-ownership platform is yielding strong revenue growth and narrowing losses, positioning it for future expansion.

item.title: Indian Auto Stocks Surge on Strong April Sales; FY27 Risks Loom
item.description: The Nifty Auto index climbed on robust April 2026 wholesale and retail sales, with passenger vehicle and tractor segments outperforming. Despite strong demand, analysts expect growth to normalize in FY27 due to potential slowdowns, geopolitical issues, and climate risks. Major automakers like Maruti Suzuki, Mahindra & Mahindra, and Tata Motors saw their stocks rise, though concerns remain about sustained momentum amid economic uncertainties.

item.title: Radico Khaitan Sets FY26 Records as Costs Threaten Margins
item.description: Radico Khaitan posted record FY26 revenue and profit, driven by its premium brands and efficient operations. The company expects further growth in FY27. However, supply chain disruptions are raising input costs, creating a risk for future profit margins even as brands perform well. Analysts remain positive with a 'Strong Buy' rating, but high stock valuation requires watching cost pressures.

item.title: CG Power: High Valuation Driven by Chip Bets, But Costs Bite Margins
item.description: CG Power & Industrial Solutions posted strong Q4 FY26 results with revenue up 25% and a large order backlog. However, significant investments in new semiconductor ventures, particularly OSAT facilities, led to a ₹38 crore hit on quarterly margins. This strategic shift towards chip manufacturing, while aligning with India's growth ambitions, places the company at a high valuation, leading to questions about returns from its costly chip expansion.

item.title: Lenskart Shares Tumble on Investor Exit Fears as IPO Lock-up Looms
item.description: Lenskart Solutions Ltd shares tumbled on Thursday, May 7, 2026, dropping 4.14 percent to Rs 482.4 amid speculation of a substantial block deal. Reports indicate that early backers, including SoftBank-backed SVF II Lightbulb (Cayman) and Abu Dhabi Investment Authority-backed Platinum Jasmine Trust, may be looking to divest approximately 4 percent of their equity. This potential stake sale is fueled by the impending expiry of the lock-in period for IPO anchor investors, raising concerns about supply overhang and near-term price pressure.

item.title: Infra.Market Lands ₹500 Cr Last Private Funding Amid IPO Caution
item.description: Construction materials platform Infra.Market is finalizing a ₹500 crore Series H funding round at a ₹25,000 crore valuation, positioning it for an upcoming IPO. This marks the company's last private capital raise as it prepares for a public listing within four to six months. The infusion aims to bolster its balance sheet, potentially reducing debt ahead of the stock market debut, a critical step given the current IPO environment.

item.title: India's Tata, JSW Pour $1 Billion Into EV Battery Tech Race
item.description: India's Tata Group and JSW Group are investing nearly $1 billion combined to develop their own electric vehicle and battery technology. This move aims to reduce reliance on China and build domestic capabilities as India's EV market grows rapidly. Tata's Agratas unit is putting over $400 million into an R&D facility for LFP and Li-Mn-Fe-P battery types. JSW Motors plans at least $500 million over five years for a research hub to adapt global technology. The companies face significant challenges, including high R&D costs, the need for foreign expertise, and potential execution risks.

item.title: Kotak Mahindra gets RBI approval for stakes in AU SFB, Federal Bank
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India has approved Kotak Mahindra Bank's plan to buy up to 9.99% of AU Small Finance Bank and Federal Bank. This allows Kotak Mahindra Group to build significant minority stakes, aiming to explore potential synergies and gather market insights from two fast-growing lenders. The approvals were confirmed in regulatory filings. Markets reacted positively, with shares of AU Small Finance Bank, Federal Bank, and Kotak Mahindra Bank seeing modest gains.

item.title: India Auto Sales Soar in April 2026 Amid Supply Chain Worries
item.description: India's auto sector reported strong year-on-year growth in April 2026 across passenger cars, two-wheelers, and three-wheelers, driven by steady demand and exports. Maruti Suzuki and Tata Motors led passenger car sales, while Hero MotoCorp and Bajaj Auto saw major gains in two-wheelers. However, ongoing global tensions and supply chain issues pose significant risks to future profits and the industry's recovery.

item.title: Bharat Forge: Q4 Profit Down 17% on Charges, FY Profit Up 19%
item.description: Bharat Forge's fourth-quarter net profit declined 17% to ₹233.45 crore, impacted by ₹98.73 crore in exceptional costs for its German subsidiary and investment impairment. Despite this quarterly drop, consolidated revenue rose 17.5% to ₹4,528.04 crore. For the full fiscal year 2026, net profit increased 19.3% to ₹1,089.4 crore on an 11.2% revenue gain to ₹16,811.65 crore. The company also recommended a final dividend of ₹6.50 per share.

item.title: Bajaj Holdings Pays ₹130 Dividend as Profit Jumps Despite Revenue Drop
item.description: Bajaj Holdings & Investment Ltd. is distributing a generous final dividend of ₹130 per equity share, including a special ₹50 payout to mark the Bajaj Group's 100th anniversary. This follows full-year results showing a strong 49% rise in net profit to ₹2,575 crore. However, profit growth occurred alongside a sharp 58% drop in revenue to ₹61 crore. The announcement highlights contrasting performance, typical for a holding company focused on investment income rather than direct sales.

item.title: Animoca Brands Pivots to AI Agents for Next Blockchain Wave
item.description: Animoca Brands, known for digital property rights, is shifting its focus from metaverse concepts to a future driven by AI agents. Chairman Yat Siu believes these AI systems, rather than humans in VR, will power the next stage of virtual economies by operating on blockchain. To boost this transition, Animoca launched a $10 million initiative for developers on its Minds AI agent platform. This signals a strategic push for blockchain as machine-to-machine infrastructure, aiming to overcome crypto's user adoption challenges. The company is also advancing plans for a Nasdaq listing through a reverse merger with Currenc Group Inc., targeted for 2026.

item.title: Hero MotoCorp's Q4 Profit Jumps 30%, EV Competition Intensifies
item.description: Hero MotoCorp reported strong Q4FY26 results, with net profit jumping 30% and revenue growing 29%, driven by a better product mix and higher sales volumes. While some analysts maintain 'Buy' ratings, others express caution due to fierce competition, especially in the electric vehicle (EV) segment where its market share is smaller than rivals.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Q4 Meets Targets Amid Rising Costs & Export Worries
item.description: Bajaj Auto posted Q4 FY26 earnings of INR 27.2 billion, matching analyst forecasts. The company maintained operating margins at 20.8%, boosted by currency tailwinds and price hikes. However, it expects slower domestic growth in FY27 and faces geopolitical risks to exports. Rising input costs will likely limit margin improvement, leading Motilal Oswal to assign a neutral rating and fair valuation.

item.title: Motilal Oswal Rates SRF 'Buy' With ₹3,400 Target Price
item.description: Motilal Oswal has begun covering SRF with a 'Buy' recommendation and a ₹3,400 price target. The brokerage highlighted strong 4QFY26 performance, showing a 14% year-on-year rise in EBIT across its Chemicals, Performance Films, and Technical Textiles segments. SRF showed strength in challenging global markets, navigating difficulties well. The outlook predicts solid revenue and profit increases through FY28.

item.title: Motilal Oswal Initiates 'Buy' on Tata Steel, Targets ₹250
item.description: Motilal Oswal has initiated a 'Buy' rating on TATA Steel, setting a target price of ₹250. The firm expects strong near-to-medium term performance for its domestic business, driven by price increases resulting from safeguard duties and fewer imports. Capacity expansions and a gradual turnaround in the EU business are also anticipated to boost overall results.

item.title: Shree Cement Margins Squeezed by Costs; Valuations Called Expensive
item.description: Shree Cement's Q4 FY26 operating performance met expectations, with EBITDA at ₹12.5 billion and adjusted PAT at ₹5.3 billion. However, rising costs caused an 11% year-over-year decline in EBITDA and a 19% drop in EBITDA per ton to ₹1,161. Operating profit margins narrowed to about 22%. Motilal Oswal reiterated a Neutral rating and a ₹26,000 price target, citing valuations as fair but not compelling. The stock has fallen 15.08% in the past year.

item.title: Alkyl Amines Stock: Premium Valuation Tests Growth Outlook
item.description: Alkyl Amines Chemicals reported steady Q4 FY26 results, with EBITDA up 4% year-over-year and margins improving to 18.3% by passing costs to customers. Volume growth was flat in FY26, but a projected 5-10% increase for FY27, driven by pharma demand and anti-dumping duties on acetonitrile, offers potential. However, the stock trades at a significant premium, nearly 44-50x earnings, well above industry averages, leading analysts to a cautious 'Neutral' rating and a year of stock underperformance.

item.title: Centrum Wealth Hires Ex-Avendus Wealth CIO Rungta to Boost Indian Market Share
item.description: Centrum Wealth is strategically appointing Saurabh Rungta, former Avendus Wealth CIO, as Deputy CEO and CIO. This move signals an aggressive bid to scale operations and enhance product strategy in India's rapidly expanding and intensely competitive high-net-worth individual and family office segments. Rungta brings over two decades of experience from leadership roles at Nuvama, Kotak, and IIFL Wealth, tasked with navigating a market grappling with consolidation and evolving client demands. The firm manages approximately ₹40,000 crore in assets across 17 locations, aiming to bolster its market position.

item.title: Container Corp India Eyes DFC Benefits Amid Valuation, Competition Concerns
item.description: India's Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) is now operational, boosting rail logistics for JNPT port. Container Corporation of India (CCI) is a key beneficiary. Motilal Oswal reiterates 'BUY' with a INR600 price target. However, CCI faces competition and its valuation is scrutinized due to past growth challenges.

item.title: India's New Senior Tax Exemption Too Narrow, Old Regime Still Preferred
item.description: The Indian government's introduction of Form 125, aimed at simplifying tax filing for senior citizens aged 75 and above, offers relief only to a highly restricted group. This exemption, which shifts tax compliance to banks, requires solely pension and interest income from a single institution. However, for most seniors, the legacy benefits of the old tax regime, including significant deductions on interest income and medical expenses, continue to offer greater financial advantage and necessitate traditional ITR filing.

item.title: Indian Auto Giants Boost Dividends, But EV Costs Pose Future Risk
item.description: India's top auto companies, like M&M and Maruti Suzuki, are set to pay large dividends for FY25-26, showing strong current profits. However, the industry must invest heavily in electric vehicles (EVs) and faces slower growth, casting doubt on whether these payouts can continue.

item.title: Pronto Raises $45M at $200M Valuation to Organize India's Gig Workers
item.description: Instant household services platform Pronto has closed a $45 million Series B round, valuing the company at $200 million. Led by Lachy Groom, the funds will scale Pronto's workforce to meet strong demand. The company aims to fix inefficiencies and job insecurity for India's informal service workers by providing structured employment, benefits, and safety nets, moving beyond typical aggregator models to organize this inconsistent sector.

item.title: India Capital Goods Index Hits Record High; Valuations Under Scrutiny
item.description: The BSE Capital Goods index achieved a new all-time high, buoyed by significant buying in power and cable companies, nearing a 2% intraday surge on Thursday. This rally, a 17% increase over two months, is partly attributed to a political shift in West Bengal favouring industrialization and increased capital expenditure. Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) and Polycab India were prominent gainers, hitting fresh highs. Analysts maintain positive outlooks for both, projecting strong order inflows for BHEL and robust growth for Polycab's wires and cables segment, driven by extensive infrastructure development and government initiatives.

item.title: Motilal Oswal Launches Contrarian Fund Amid Volatile Markets
item.description: Motilal Oswal Mutual Fund has launched the Motilal Oswal Contra Fund, a new equity fund aiming for long-term growth by buying undervalued stocks. The fund will be open for subscriptions from May 8-22, 2026, with a minimum investment of ₹500. It enters a challenging market marked by global uncertainty, foreign investor selling, and high stock prices, which could affect its performance.

item.title: India Inc Earnings: AI Hits IT, Consumer Sectors Gain Amid Inflation
item.description: India Inc's March quarter (Q4FY26) earnings revealed a mixed performance. The IT sector faces falling stock prices due to AI disruption fears, with the Nifty IT index down 25% this year as foreign investors exit. Meanwhile, inflation risks from the West Asia conflict and rising input costs persist. However, consumer sectors like autos, FMCG, and real estate show strong demand, while Global Capability Centers (GCCs) expand, creating new tech growth.

item.title: AirAsia Orders 150 Airbus A220s in $19B Bet to Fight Fuel Costs
item.description: AirAsia finalized a $19 billion order for 150 Airbus A220-300 jets, its largest ever commitment to the aircraft. Starting deliveries in 2028, the airline will use the A220's efficient economics to make money on smaller routes and combat rising fuel costs exacerbated by global tensions, aiming for future growth.

item.title: Coal India Stock Falls as Govt Plans Stake Sale, Overrides Strong Q4 Results
item.description: Coal India Ltd. shares declined amid reports of a potential 3-4% stake sale by the government, aiming to raise around Rs 10,000 crore. This news overshadowed strong fiscal fourth-quarter results, which showed an 11.15% profit increase to Rs 10,839 crore and a 5% revenue rise to Rs 46,490 crore. Despite positive operational results and a low P/E ratio (around 9.5x) significantly below industry peers, the stock faced selling pressure from concerns over increased share supply and potential dilution.

item.title: Paytm, Bajaj Auto Jump on Strong Q4 Earnings; Market Flat
item.description: Benchmark indices hovered near flatlines as investors focused on sharp stock-specific movements driven by Q4FY26 earnings. One 97 Communications (Paytm) and Bajaj Auto posted significant gains, hitting highs on strong results, robust profits, and shareholder returns. CG Power, Meesho, and Hero MotoCorp also recorded notable advances, while United Breweries and Blue Star showed mixed performance amidst sector-wide earnings reports.
